title: Cache variables and the option command
description: Cache variables persist across configures and are set with -D or option(), while normal variables live only for the current configure run.
body: Use when deciding how a build setting should be exposed and remembered. Do not use a normal set() for something users must override from the command line. Cache variables are stored in CMakeCache.txt and survive reconfigure, settable via -D or the option() command for booleans; normal variables reset each run. Inspect cached values with cmake -LAH. Validation signals include a user toggling a feature with -DOPTION=ON and the choice persisting. Risk boundary: a stale cached value can override intent silently, so document options and clear the cache when fundamentals change.
